description: You are an 11th-grade physics student completing a laboratory exercise on the refraction of light using the Sugar Learning Platform. Identify an unknown transparent material using the dataset of laser angle measurements at /home/ga/Documents/optics_data.csv. Calculate the index of refraction for each entry using Snell's Law (n = sin(theta_i) / sin(theta_r)) and determine the average index of refraction across the dataset. Save your calculation script to /home/ga/Documents/calculate_n.py. Identify the material by comparing your average result to standard values for Water, Crown Glass, and Diamond. Create a lab report stating the calculated average index of refraction (rounded to two decimal places) and the identified material name. Save the report to /home/ga/Documents/optics_report.odt.
